element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Single-player campaign begins with player controlling ECO35-2 through fixed progression
Each subsequent droid introduced with pre-rendered 3D sequence and weakness analysis
Fixed order progression with each adversary more difficult than last; player learns combat through escalation
Ultimate antagonist representing narrative climax; most difficult encounter in single-player mode
Five driods from campaign available for selection; special cheat enables Supervisor; arcade version expands roster to eighteen characters

typical_tension_triggers: Progressive difficulty scaling across six levels
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Linear escalation with fixed opponent progression; tension builds consistently through campaign as difficulty increases with each level; cinematic interludes provide pacing breaks

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Cheat code enables Supervisor in versus mode (hidden content unlock)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction through first opponent (Sentry); baseline difficulty for learning combat mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 2-4 with escalating opponent AI and droid complexity; progressive mastery required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Levels 5-6 featuring significantly harder adversaries culminating in Supervisor boss fight
notable_spikes: Level 6: Supervisor droid encounter represents final difficulty spike
Fixed progression order means no difficulty adjustment; player must overcome each prescribed opponent

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player mode features mandatory opponent order; each droid must be defeated sequentially before advancing. No branching paths or optional encounters.
Each opponent is introduced via pre-rendered 3D sequence followed by weakness analysis, creating pacing rhythm and narrative framing between encounters
Two-player mode allows character selection from encountered opponents plus hidden Supervisor; multiple rounds (2-7) per session for competitive balance
